title: hieracium sagittatum subsp. marginellum
intro: Hieracium sagittatum subsp. marginellum, also known as a subspecies of the Arrowhead Hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of Hieracium sagittatum subsp. marginellum typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, then using them to make teas, infusions, or infusing them in oil for topical applications.
